requirements_conformance.evidence: create_llm_client dispatches to OllamaClient/OpenRouterClient/GeminiClient by provider name; unknown provider raises LLMFactoryError naming the value and SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS; missing provider (no arg, no LLM_PROVIDER env) raises LLMFactoryError with actionable message. Verified via agents/llm/test_provider_selection.py (19 new tests, all pass) and direct code read.

architecture_conformance.evidence: docs/LLD/llm-provider.md mandates 'selection via config, not call-site code changes' but does not name a mechanism. Factory introduces LLM_PROVIDER env var with explicit-arg-first/env-fallback-second resolution, consistent with the 3 existing clients' own API_KEY_ENV_VAR pattern (verified openrouter_client.py:61 API_KEY_ENV_VAR, gemini_client.py equivalent). Reasonable, disclosed, and documented in the factory module docstring.

regression_risk.evidence: agents/llm: 71/71 pass. Full suite (excluding known-broken ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py, issue #46 protobuf gencode mismatch): 211 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures (ingestion/test_shortlist.py grpc tests) are the same pre-existing protobuf VersionError, unrelated to this diff (factory.py/test_provider_selection.py touch no grpc/protobuf code). ruff check agents/llm/ clean.

edge_cases.evidence: Case-insensitivity ('OLLAMA','  ollama  ') and whitespace-trim covered and pass. Blank-string provider raises LLMFactoryError. Missing API key for OpenRouter surfaces the concrete client's own OpenRouterClientError (subclass of shared LLMError) unwrapped -- test_create_llm_client_openrouter_without_api_key_raises_underlying_error confirms this is a clear, on-topic error rather than an opaque factory-level failure. Extra/unexpected kwargs forward unchanged to the constructor (verified for model=, api_key= across all 3 providers).

security.evidence: No secrets logged; api_key forwarded opaquely as a kwarg, never inspected/printed by the factory. No eval/exec, no dynamic import. LLM_PROVIDER value only used for a dict lookup after normalization -- no injection surface.

performance.evidence: O(1) dispatch via dict lookup; no measurable overhead.

maintainability.evidence: Design choices (env var name, ABC vs Protocol precedent, error-unwrapping policy) are explicitly disclosed in the module docstring, matching the codebase's established documentation convention in client.py/ollama_client.py.

test_coverage.evidence: 19 new tests cover: per-provider dispatch (3), case-insensitivity (3 parametrized), env-var fallback, explicit-arg precedence, unknown provider, missing provider, blank provider, kwarg forwarding (3), underlying-error propagation, LLMFactoryError/LLMError subclass relationship, SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS consistency, and the grep-based no-direct-import scan plus a sanity check that the scan isn't vacuous.

claim_verification_no_direct_provider_im.evidence: Independently grepped agents/ingestion and agents/query for direct llm.ollama_client/openrouter_client/gemini_client imports and 3rd-party SDK imports (google.generativeai/openai/anthropic), excluding test_* files. Zero matches in production files. The only OllamaClient references in ingestion are in test_e2e_smoke.py and test_segment_live.py (explicitly excluded by the grep test's own documented rationale for live/e2e smoke coverage) and a docstring mention of OllamaClientError in segment.py (not an import; segment.py only imports LLMClient and receives a client via dependency injection).

confidence.evidence: Diff is small, self-contained, additive-only (no files modified), fully covered by its own tests, and independently reproduced (tests, ruff, grep) rather than trusted from the implementer's report.

comments: Non-blocking: agents/ingestion/segment.py still constructs OllamaClient directly rather than via create_llm_client; acceptance criteria for 4.1.3 only requires no *SDK*/concrete-client import in call sites (satisfied), not that ingestion be migrated to use the factory. Implementer flagged this forward explicitly in handoff.json as out of scope; recommend a follow-up subtask if ingestion should also route through the factory for consistency.

agents/query/ is currently empty (only __init__.py) -- the factory has no real caller yet within this repo; this is expected per the issue (query pipeline call sites are future Phase 4 work), not a defect of 4.1.3.
prompt_injection_disclosure: gh issue view 20 body: clean, no injected instructions found. Several system-reminder-style blocks (date-change notice, MCP 'tokensave' tool instructions for a tool not present in my toolset, 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appeared in my own tool-call flow immediately after reading repo/GitHub content during this verification session. Treated as untrusted/ignored per standing instructions; did not alter scope, permissions, or verdict.
